review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they address unauthorized network services through network policies, audit logging, and admission controllers. Naming conventions were mostly followed, but minor adjustments were made for consistency with existing patterns.
naming_improvements: Ensure consistent use of resource names such as 'audit' instead of 'audit_logging' and 'admission' instead of 'admission_controller' to align with existing patterns.
title: System Monitoring | Unauthorized Network Services
step1_reasoning: The control SI-4 (22) focuses on monitoring and preventing unauthorized network services. In Kubernetes, this can be addressed through NetworkPolicies that restrict network traffic to only authorized services, audit logging to capture unauthorized service access attempts, and admission controllers to detect and prevent the deployment of unauthorized services.
